name: add-dashboard
tagline: Installs and wires a local NanoClaw monitoring dashboard (npm package + pusher) into index.ts, .env, and the running service.
verdict: Solid installer skill: concrete commands throughout, bundled AST-checked tests verify the wiring edit, and network traffic stays local (127.0.0.1) exactly as described — no scope mismatch or injection issues found. Weak points: the npm dependency is installed unpinned, the index.ts edit and .env append have no re-run guard (could duplicate on a second.

highlights.summary: `pnpm install @nanoco/nanoclaw-dashboard` has no version pin; legitimate first-party scoped package but unpinned to latest.
Step 3 (index.ts block) and step 4 (.env vars) have no existence check; re-running would duplicate the import/call or the env lines.
vitest and systemctl/launchctl are invoked in step 5 with no Requirements section or availability check.
